title: View Pending Messages And Recent Alarm History In The Message Panel
summary: Use the map monitor summary area message panel to view pending messages containing active errors or warnings and review recent alarm history described as approximately the past 24 to 48 hours.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to check the map monitor summary area for current pending messages, active errors or warnings, or recent alarm history visible in the message panel.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to interpret alarm meanings beyond what is shown in the panel.
Do not use this procedure as a corrective action or recovery procedure because the source only supports viewing and reviewing the panel contents.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the map monitor summary area, open or view the message panel, and review visible pending messages and recent alarm history.

safety_notes: This source supports viewing information in the HMI message panel only.
Do not infer corrective actions, alarm meanings, or recovery steps beyond what is explicitly shown in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the map monitor summary area

access_required: Operator access to the map monitor summary area
system_preconditions: The map monitor interface is available
The message panel is visible or can be opened from the map monitor summary area

visual_references.description: Map monitor summary area reference showing the open message panel and describing pending messages with active errors or warnings plus recent alarm history.
Map Monitor message panel view on the right side of the interface with related status indicators.

steps.title: Locate the message panel in the map monitor summary area
steps.purpose: Identify the correct HMI area where pending messages and alarm history are displayed.
steps.instruction: Go to the map monitor summary area and locate the open message panel shown in the training material.
steps.expected_result: The message panel location in the map monitor summary area is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can visually identify the message panel in the map monitor summary area.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot find the message panel location in the map monitor summary area.

actions.label: Locate message panel
actions.exact_action: Locate the open message panel in the map monitor summary area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The map monitor summary area showing the open message panel location and its purpose.
The right-side Map Monitor message panel and related status area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the message panel cannot be located and the source provides no further navigation detail.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The first one is the open message panel...
Slide content appears to describe the Map Monitor message panel on the right side of the interface.

steps.title: View pending active messages
steps.purpose: Check for currently visible pending messages that contain active errors or warnings.
steps.instruction: Open the message panel if needed and view the pending messages shown there for active errors or warnings.
steps.expected_result: Pending messages containing active errors or warnings are visible in the panel.
steps.healthy_condition: The panel displays pending messages and the operator can review any active errors or warnings shown.
steps.failure_condition: The panel does not show pending messages when they are expected, or the operator cannot view the active errors or warnings.

actions.label: Open or view message panel
actions.exact_action: Open the message panel to view pending messages containing active errors or warnings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The message panel area described as showing pending messages containing active errors or warnings.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the panel does not show expected messages and the source does not provide further recovery steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: pending messages containing active errors or warnings

steps.title: Review recent alarm history
steps.purpose: Review recent alarm entries available from the message panel.
steps.instruction: Review the alarm history shown in the message panel for alarms generated over approximately the past 24 to 48 hours.
steps.expected_result: Recent alarm history entries are visible for review in the panel.
steps.healthy_condition: The panel shows alarm history entries consistent with the recent period described in the source.
steps.failure_condition: Alarm history is not visible when expected, or the visible history does not appear available for review.

actions.label: Review alarm history
actions.exact_action: Review the alarm history shown in the panel for alarms generated over approximately the past 24 to 48 hours.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The message panel description indicating alarm history for roughly the past 24 to 48 hours.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if expected alarm history is not shown and the source does not provide additional troubleshooting.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: as well as the history of the alarms that it generated over maybe like the past 24-48 hours

steps.title: Record visible messages and alarm entries
steps.purpose: Capture the currently visible information for follow-up or escalation.
steps.instruction: Record the active errors, warnings, or recent alarm entries that are visible in the message panel.
steps.expected_result: The visible active errors, warnings, or recent alarm entries are documented by the operator.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator has a record of the visible panel contents for communication or escalation.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot capture or document the visible panel contents.

actions.label: Document visible entries
actions.exact_action: Record the active errors, warnings, or recent alarm entries that are visible in the panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The message panel contents that show pending messages and recent alarm history to be recorded.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the panel does not show expected messages or history and the source does not provide further recovery steps.
Stop short of assigning alarm meaning or corrective action beyond what is shown in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The open message panel shows pending messages containing active errors or warnings as well as recent alarm history.
Candidate is limited to viewing and reviewing message panel contents because the source does not provide corrective actions.

success_criteria: The operator can locate the message panel in the map monitor summary area.
Pending messages containing active errors or warnings are visible in the panel.
Recent alarm history for approximately the past 24 to 48 hours can be reviewed.
Visible active messages or alarm entries are recorded for follow-up.
healthy_conditions: The message panel is visible in the map monitor summary area.
Pending messages can be viewed in the panel.
Alarm history is available in the panel for recent alarms.
failure_conditions: The message panel cannot be located.
Expected pending messages are not shown.
Expected recent alarm history is not shown.
The source does not provide further recovery or interpretation steps.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the panel does not show expected messages or history and the source does not provide further recovery steps.
Escalate when the operator cannot locate or access the message panel from the map monitor summary area.
Do not infer alarm meanings or corrective actions beyond what is shown in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Open message panel in the map monitor summary area showing pending messages and recent alarm history.
Map Monitor message panel on the right side of the interface.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The first one is the open message panel... pending messages containing active errors or warnings... as well as the history of the alarms that it generated over maybe like the past 24-48 hours.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact click sequence or control name for opening the message panel.
The source does not provide corrective actions for any errors, warnings, or alarms shown.
The source does not define how to export, save, or formally log the visible entries.
The source describes the alarm history window approximately as the past 24 to 48 hours rather than a fixed retention value.
